title: BEM-917 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM917_WORKER_UNIFIED_PROTOCOL_V2_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-917 WORKER UNIFIED PROTOCOL V2 REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- Operator remarks are valid and must be incorporated into the unified BEM-916 protocol.
- Telegram must be configured to the General Director object, not to an undefined or generic curator.
- The receiving point is EL-CUR-GD-001, the Curator element inside OBJ-GD-001. This preserves the hierarchy: Operator -> Telegram -> General Director Curator -> General Director -> Director/Worker through managed channel.
- Reports must be controlled by operator-selected periodicity. Default period is 1 hour.
- Later report period changes must be stored as configuration and must not create prohibited schedule triggers without explicit operator approval.
- All Telegram reports must follow the reporting canon, including progress percentages. This fixes the previously identified gap where the operator could not understand where the system stopped.
- The updated protocol must remain unified. BEM-917 is a v2 update of BEM-916, not a separate Telegram protocol.
Autonomous scope:
- Protocol update, schema, command grammar, report template, default period config, deterministic parser/selftest design.
Operator gate:
- Real bot token or secret updates, production webhook mutation, new schedule trigger, always-on daemon model, issue #31 writes, real pilot selection.
Recommendation:
- Approve unified protocol v2 with notes.
- Status: UNIFIED_PROTOCOL_V2_APPROVED_WITH_OPERATOR_GATES.
No issue comments.
